title: Security Modes
goal: Implement configurable Howy security modes 0 (plaintext), 1 (cached AEAD), and 2 (ephemeral AEAD using a user-type key in a service-private session keyring), reserve mode 3 for future hardware isolation, and add optional prompt-before-capture confirmation with secure new-install bootstrap defaults, backward-compatible explicit upgrades, low authentication latency, and daemon-owned storage.
scope: Stable configurable embedding modes 0 plaintext, 1 cached AEAD, 2 ephemeral AEAD, with mode 3 reserved.
Explicit insecure mode 0, secure disabled new-install bootstrap, and backward-compatible absent-field behavior.
Frozen HOWYENC1/HOWYPLN1 formats, exact mode namespaces, one-epoch v1 policy, stable enrollment IDs, generation CAS, mutation table, and atomic durability.
Daemon-owned live/batch enrollment, presence, authentication load, list, remove, clear, diagnostics, credentials, shutdown, and exhaustive authorization.
Mode 1 systemd TPM/host credential, guarded daemon key, bounded decrypt-on-load cache, and zero warm-path storage crypto.

Mode 2 actual ExecStart TPM launcher, `user`-type key in `KEY_SPEC_SESSION_KEYRING`, possessor-only permissions, sanitized exact serial handoff, transient per-operation key read, userspace AEAD, request-local zeroizing records, fail-closed child detachment, and decrypt/camera overlap.
Optional prompt-before-capture confirmation with same-connection two-phase protocol, lazy camera access, active HUP cancellation, honest claims, and PAM compatibility.
Transactional fresh install/provisioning, byte-preserving upgrades/reinstalls, explicit namespace reactivation/re-registration choices, recovery, packaging, and operator guidance.
Cross-feature security, fuzz, compatibility, performance, packaging, and operational validation.
nonGoals: Implement mode 3 or claim TPM2 can bulk-decrypt embedding records.
Implement gesture, nod, blink, biometric liveness, or presentation-attack detection.
Remove mode 0 or prevent users from explicitly choosing insecure operation.
Automatically import plaintext enrollment records into encrypted modes.
Add TPM unseal commands to the per-authentication path.
Protect against live root, kernel compromise, daemon code execution, or malicious same-UID protocol clients.
Implement TPM NV rollback counters in the initial format.
Build a hardware-specific HIP/DMA-BUF inference pipeline.
Change recognition models, thresholds, camera exposure behavior, or execution providers.
Guarantee password fallback independently of PAM stack control configuration.
Silently overwrite existing configuration during package upgrades or local reinstall.
Permit daemon child processes to inherit possession of the mode-2 keyring.
constraints: All delivered features are optional and independently configurable; explicit insecure mode 0 remains fully supported.
Mode numbers are stable identifiers, not assurance levels. Documentation must not imply that higher numbers are categorically more secure.

New-install templates choose a disabled mode-1 bootstrap plus prompt confirmation; existing configs with absent new fields preserve mode 0/prompt-off. A wholly missing daemon config fails with a generation/migration instruction rather than silently choosing a mode.

Configured encrypted modes fail closed on missing keys, corrupt records, wrong namespaces, unsupported epochs, or incompatible formats; there is no silent downgrade. Users may explicitly provision mode 0.
Mode 3 is reserved future work only. TPM2 is not treated as a bulk AEAD engine; future hardware-isolated designs require separate research and approval.
AF_ALG is explicitly rejected and must not be implemented or enabled in service sandboxes.

Mode 1 unseals through a systemd TPM/host encrypted credential once and retains a guarded daemon key; mode 2 unseals once through the actual ExecStart launcher into a `user`-type key linked only to the service-private session keyring and reads it transiently per operation.
Prompt confirmation is a supported-client user-intent interaction, not biometric liveness, PAD, trusted-path attestation, or proof against malicious same-UID clients.
The daemon must not probe, open, configure, or stream the camera before a valid prompt commit when confirmation is enabled; the camera never streams while waiting for human input.
Enrollment and all storage mutations remain root-only. Authentication, credential operations, and enrollment-presence checks permit root or the matching UID under the exhaustive authorization table.
All storage modes use daemon-owned enrollment and CRUD; raw embeddings do not cross normal CLI IPC.
Preserve provider-agnostic inference, current password-fallback-compatible PAM return semantics, and no continuous camera operation.
No packages, live TPM keys, PAM/systemd changes, production camera/model runs, enrollment deletion, commits, or deployment without explicit approval.
Do not overwrite or absorb unrelated uncommitted changes. Snapshot the baseline diff before each implementation slice.
Documentation changes are limited to necessary README/operator sections unless the user separately approves additional committed documents; report stale historical docs explicitly.
Each implementation slice follows code-writer, adversarial code-checker, writer fixes, rerun evidence, and an explicit gate before the next slice.

steps.title: Separate defaults and provenance
steps.target: crates/howy-common/src/config.rs, config.toml, howy.config, CLI config generation
steps.action: Create distinct legacy deserialization defaults, explicit fresh-install secure template generation, and loaded-config provenance. Missing new fields map to mode 0/off; fresh templates write mode 1/confirm; a wholly missing daemon config fails with a generation instruction.
steps.validation: Passed: 4 targeted common config tests; CLI config command/build; touched-file rustfmt and diff checks. Full fmt blocked only by pre-existing unrelated files.

steps.title: Define security configuration contract
steps.target: common config types and status/doctor schemas
steps.action: Define stable numeric embedding modes 0/1/2/3, independent presence off/confirm policy, mode-specific required fields, strict validation, and precise unsupported/re-registration errors.
steps.validation: Passed: 15 targeted common config tests, targeted rustfmt, and diff check; legacy fresh template remains inactive and secure bootstrap is explicit.

steps.title: Implement frozen record specification
steps.target: common storage module, workplan normative specification, golden-vector fixtures
steps.action: Implement the frozen HOWYENC1/HOWYPLN1 layouts, exact NSS canonical-name mapping, byte-framed legacy hashes, random-nonce policy without an unenforceable counter claim, exact offsets/AAD/payload/limits, model digest, and trailing-byte rules.
steps.validation: Passed: 26 storage tests, 44 total common tests, cargo check, targeted rustfmt, and diff check; exact pinned dependencies recorded.

steps.title: Implement exact namespaces and transitions
steps.target: paths, storage backends, transition tests
steps.action: Implement exact namespaces `/etc/howy/models/<user>.bin|.json`, `/etc/howy/models/mode1/<user>.hye`, and `/etc/howy/models/mode2/<user>.hye`, with mode directories 0700 and files 0600. Implement the frozen transition matrix: only selected namespace is active; selecting a prior mode intentionally reactivates still-valid records; no automatic import/delete/overwrite; explicit clear/purge remains operator-controlled.
steps.validation: Passed: 33 storage tests, 2 path tests, cargo check, targeted rustfmt, and diff check.

steps.title: Implement v1 epoch and generation rules
steps.target: record format, key config, mutation logic
steps.action: Implement one active key epoch per encrypted mode. Initial epoch is 1; every successful mutation increments record generation from 1 with checked overflow. Changing epoch rejects old records and requires re-registration; no multi-key reads or automatic re-encryption.
steps.validation: Covered by config validation and storage tests for zero/expected epoch binding, initial generation, checked increment, and overflow; backend application remains explicitly validated in later phases.

steps.title: Define storage and concurrency contracts
steps.target: common face records and daemon storage/cache interfaces
steps.action: Introduce a daemon StorageBackend and lease contract supporting cached immutable and request-local zeroizing records, stable enrollment IDs, persisted record generation, and mutation compare-and-swap.
steps.validation: Passed: 43 storage tests, 61 total common tests, cargo check, targeted rustfmt, and diff check; zeroize 1.9.0 alloc-only pinned.

steps.title: Review contracts slice
steps.target: contracts diff, format, tests, migration matrix
steps.action: Conduct adversarial review of the contracts slice.
steps.validation: Completed: no critical findings; two major zeroization blockers and five lower findings recorded.

steps.title: Fix contracts findings
steps.target: contracts slice
steps.action: Apply review fixes and rerun contract checks.
steps.validation: Passed final targeted review: no remaining findings. 57 storage tests, 75 total common tests, cargo check, rustfmt, feature-tree and diff checks pass.

steps.title: Implement versioned plaintext mode
steps.target: common face storage, plaintext backend, legacy decoder
steps.action: Preserve legacy `.bin`/`.json` reads. Mode-0 new writes use frozen HOWYPLN1 at the existing `.bin` path. Legacy entries receive deterministic IDs/generation tokens for list/CAS and convert atomically to HOWYPLN1 only on authorized mutation.
steps.validation: Passed: 75 common tests, 14 daemon storage tests, daemon cargo check, targeted rustfmt, diff check; no production path touched.

steps.title: Enforce exhaustive request authorization
steps.target: protobuf request surface, daemon peer authorization, public/root status schemas
steps.action: Implement the exhaustive authorization table in the detailed plan for Authenticate/Begin/Commit/Cancel, Enroll, EnrollBatch, Detect, Ping, public Info, root SecurityInfo/Reload/List/Remove/Clear/Shutdown, CheckCredential, and RevokeCredential. Resolve all target usernames through NSS, including root requests, before side effects.
steps.validation: Passed: 11 authorization tests, 31 server tests, 2 protocol tests, daemon cargo check, targeted rustfmt and diff check. Public Info model paths are empty; root SecurityInfo remains next-step scope.

steps.title: Make daemon own every storage transaction
steps.target: proto, protocol, daemon, CLI, PAM, batch enrollment
steps.action: Move live and batch enrollment commit, enrollment presence, authentication load, list, stable-ID/generation remove, generation-checked clear, reload, and security diagnostics behind daemon storage transactions. Live/batch inference occurs before a short compare-and-swap commit so long capture/processing does not retain record plaintext/key material.
steps.validation: Passed reported targeted suites and workspace check; no active handler or CLI CRUD path directly writes UserModels; metadata-only enrollment IPC and CAS operations implemented.

steps.title: Close diagnostic embedding leaks
steps.target: DetectResult protocol and CLI diagnostics
steps.action: Make detect/preview behavior explicit: keep it root-only and remove raw embedding output unless a separately reviewed diagnostic requirement proves necessary.
steps.validation: Passed: tag 7 reserved, detector-only response, old field ignored/not re-emitted, 6 protocol and 34 server tests, daemon check and diff check.

steps.title: Define cache coherence and memory budget
steps.target: daemon model cache and memory admission
steps.action: Adopt daemon-only write coherence: no per-auth filesystem stat in cached mode; CRUD synchronously replaces/invalidates entries; external modification requires explicit reload/restart. Account cache entries, outstanding leases, and transient buffers against one hard plaintext-memory budget.
steps.validation: Passed: 25 daemon storage tests, 34 server tests, 6 main tests, 59 common storage tests, workspace check, targeted rustfmt and diff check.

steps.title: Review daemon storage slice

steps.title: Implement safe PAM confirmation
steps.target: crates/howy-pam/src/lib.rs
steps.action: Implement checked PAM echo-on confirmation handling for Enter/OK, callback status, PAM_SILENT, cancellation, EOF, null/overlong responses, allocation cleanup, and panic containment.
steps.validation: Passed final review with no findings: 13 debug/release tests; ABI, ownership, cleanup, policy, fallback, and exported panic containment verified.

steps.title: Add two-phase prompt protocol
steps.target: proto, protocol, IPC connection state machine
steps.action: Replace confirmation-enabled one-shot auth with a versioned same-connection Begin→Prompt→Commit/Cancel protocol. Cancel is valid only while pending. Commit consumes pending state; no later request frames are valid, and old/new compatibility fails to password without camera access.
steps.validation: Passed final adversarial review: frozen wire, tag-21 no-downgrade, real framed coordinator, sequencing/final ownership, timeout application, and inactive runtime boundaries verified.

steps.title: Implement prompt transaction state
steps.target: daemon transaction manager
steps.action: Implement bounded pending and active transaction states. Pending tokens bind daemon instance, peer UID, username, client nonce, and expiry; commit atomically creates active cancellation/deadline state shared by camera and storage work.
steps.validation: Passed final adversarial gate: no findings; 18 focused state and 192 daemon tests verify token/state/capacity/deadline/snapshot/zeroization/lifecycle semantics.

steps.title: Move all camera access after confirmation
steps.target: daemon startup, server, camera profile lifecycle
steps.action: Refactor camera profile probing to be lazy. With confirmation enabled, daemon startup, socket activation, BeginAuth, pending wait, cancellation, and expiry must perform no camera probe/open/configure/start operation.
steps.validation: Passed final adversarial gate: no findings; 212 daemon/392 workspace tests verify zero pre-Commit events, retry/concurrency/cleanup, and prompt-off/enrollment behavior.

steps.title: Add active HUP and deadline cancellation
steps.target: daemon connection worker, cancellation tokens, camera/storage tasks, PAM timeouts
steps.action: After commit, run auth work in a subordinate task while the connection worker monitors HUP/EOF and daemon shutdown. Compose admission, first-frame, scan, storage, cleanup, and PAM timeout budgets; HUP/EOF/shutdown/deadline cancels both camera and storage branches.
steps.validation: Passed final adversarial gate: no findings; 405 workspace tests verify cancellation/fail-stop/cache/deadline/response/race semantics.

steps.title: Add prompt policy enforcement
steps.target: PAM client policy, daemon authorization, prompt UX
steps.action: Enforce allowed PAM services, local-only policy, and unsupported-context refusal to password flow before camera access. Document and test service-name spoofability as a claim limitation.
steps.validation: Passed final review: no findings; 20 PAM debug/release and 418 workspace tests verify deadlines, fallback, presentation, policy, no-downgrade, cancellation, and zeroization.

steps.title: Implement mode 1 key lifecycle
steps.target: daemon crypto/storage startup and systemd unit
steps.action: Add systemd TPM credential configuration and a host-key fallback using documented credential search paths only. Read exact credential bytes from CREDENTIALS_DIRECTORY. The daemon never invokes systemd-creds. Wipe credentials on every error and panic path.
steps.validation: Passed final adversarial gate: no findings; 467 workspace tests plus package/installer/sysusers/systemd/key/child/model validations.

steps.title: Implement cached AEAD backend

steps.action: Implement mode 1 encrypt/decrypt storage, boot-time population, guarded key ownership, hard memory admission, atomic writes, cache synchronization, and reload. No per-auth filesystem stat, AEAD, HKDF, TPM, or protobuf embedding occurs on warm hits.

steps.action: Make fresh install, upgrade, local reinstall and explicit mode 0 transactional. Fresh mode 1 stages key/config, runs a storage-only readiness check with the candidate credential in a transient unit, installs a disabled verified candidate, and requires an explicit enable step. Preserve existing configs/namespaces by default.

steps.validation: Warm storage p99 is no more than max(0.25 ms, baseline+5%); instrumentation proves no crypto on hits; total post-commit p95/p99 regression is within approved confidence bounds.

steps.title: Prove private-session-keyring lifecycle
steps.target: disposable VM/vTPM feasibility harness only
steps.action: In a VM/vTPM or separately approved disposable host, prototype the actual ExecStart launcher: TPM-unseal a 32-byte secret, add a `user`-type key to `KEY_SPEC_SESSION_KEYRING`, set desired `KEY_POS_VIEW|KEY_POS_READ` with owner/group/other zero, zero launcher buffers, pass a sanitized exact decimal serial, exec howyd, and read by exact serial after exec.
steps.validation: Evidence covers TPM unseal, exact type/description/serial, narrowest sufficient mask, exec inheritance, malformed handoff, restart, revoke, and absence of a persistent plaintext key file.

steps.title: Prove session-keyring isolation
steps.target: VM systemd harness, camera FFmpeg spawn, generic child-spawn helper
steps.action: Test isolation from sibling root services and all child processes. FFmpeg and every future external child must join a new empty session keyring in a fail-closed pre-exec hook and receive no serial metadata; mode 2 is rejected if this cannot be guaranteed.
steps.validation: Sibling units and spawned helpers cannot read or possess the key; howyd workers can; detachment failure aborts child launch; restart receives a distinct private session.

steps.title: Collect mode 2 feasibility evidence
steps.target: VM/vTPM feasibility harness and dependency report
steps.action: Collect evidence—not the final decision—for an actual ExecStart launcher using a systemd private session keyring, a `user`-type key linked to `KEY_SPEC_SESSION_KEYRING`, desired key permissions `KEY_POS_VIEW|KEY_POS_READ` with owner/group/other zero, sanitized exact serial handoff, and transient key read after exec.
steps.validation: Evidence covers whether the desired mask is sufficient or documents the narrowest required mask, plus key type/description verification and key-read latency.

steps.title: Implement private-session-keyring launcher
steps.target: launcher, provisioning command, mode2-specific unit integration
steps.action: Implement the actual ExecStart launcher selected by the feasibility gate. It links a `user`-type key only into `KEY_SPEC_SESSION_KEYRING`, applies the approved possessor-only permission mask, constructs a sanitized environment containing only a nonsecret decimal serial plus required daemon variables, execs howyd, and never uses ExecStartPre or an ambient key search.
steps.validation: Exact key type/description/serial/mask checks, malformed serial rejection, exec/restart/revoke tests, sibling isolation, and no persistent plaintext key file pass.

steps.title: Implement complete transient mode 2 transactions
steps.target: mode2 backend, keyring wrapper, every storage/diagnostic operation
steps.action: Implement one operation-scoped transient key/plaintext transaction for mode-2 authentication, metadata list, live/batch append commit, remove, generation-checked clear, and authenticated VerifyRecord. Define enrollment presence as bounded outer-header candidate only, public health as backend readiness only, and reload as key/outer-header revalidation without plaintext cache.
steps.validation: Tests cover every classified operation, candidate-presence corruption after confirmation, CAS conflicts, memory admission, cancellation, panic, revoke, wrong key metadata, and no long-lived daemon key/cache.

steps.title: Implement ephemeral data lifecycle for all operations
steps.target: mode2 storage leases and mutation transactions
steps.action: Implement request-local zeroizing leases and operation buffers under the hard plaintext budget. Authentication reuses one lease across frames; list returns metadata then drops; mutations decrypt-modify-encrypt within the short commit transaction; clear authenticates expected generation before unlinking.
steps.validation: Auth/list/append/remove/clear success, conflict, timeout, cancellation, memory pressure, panic, and shutdown all release permits and zero sensitive buffers.

steps.title: Overlap mode 2 decrypt with camera startup
steps.target: daemon active transaction state machine
steps.action: After optional prompt commit and camera admission, run camera startup and key-read/decrypt/validation concurrently. Join before matching; decrypt failure stops camera, camera failure cancels/zeroizes storage, peer HUP cancels both.
steps.validation: Deterministic tests cover every completion order and prove TPM unseal never occurs per auth and no plaintext exists while awaiting prompt/admission.

reviewFindings.title: Importer and daemon staged-BMP size envelopes differ
reviewFindings.detail: Importer permits normalized BMP outputs larger than daemon's 8 MiB staged per-file cap. Align during final integration; normal 640x480 Python workflow is unaffected.

reviewFindings.title: Mode 1 key and cached AEAD review findings
reviewFindings.detail: All Mode 1 key/cache blocker/major findings were fixed and passed final adversarial review; detailed history remains in workplan notes.

reviewFindings.title: Provisioning rollback snapshot lacks file metadata
reviewFindings.detail: Prior file snapshot stores bytes plus opaque metadata hash but no restorable type/uid/gid/mode/nlink. Add canonical versioned metadata snapshot and bind hash for deterministic rollback.

reviewFindings.title: Cleanup admission is not descriptor-bound
reviewFindings.detail: Cleanup classifier accepts a boolean hash match without transaction/path/descriptor metadata binding. Require expected receipt identity and observed path/hash/type/uid/gid/mode/nlink exact match.

reviewFindings.title: Enabled config can exceed provisioning size cap
reviewFindings.detail: true→false patch can exceed MAX_CONFIG_BYTES. Add checked output length and boundary test.

reviewFindings.title: Readiness count and byte totals can be inconsistent
reviewFindings.detail: Readiness permits impossible record-count/ciphertext-byte tuples. Bind result to validated fingerprint and enforce checked per-record aggregate bound.

reviewFindings.title: Credential parser lacks independent positive fixtures
reviewFindings.detail: Accepted credential tests self-generate fixtures from implementation assumptions. Add independent systemd v261 host/TPM/combined positive fixtures and malformed/evidence vectors.

reviewFindings.title: Staged artifact naming is misclassified
reviewFindings.detail: Classifier misses actual `.staged.<suffix>` names. Add complete transaction artifact forms.

reviewFindings.title: Strong readiness flags are not strictly paired
reviewFindings.detail: Strong readiness now requires --storage-readiness-only, --config, and --verify-records together; header-only accepts neither candidate flag.

reviewFindings.title: Verifier deadline excludes setup/output and key drop
reviewFindings.detail: One 120-second absolute deadline now covers identity/config/key/model/namespace/serialization/output work; guarded key and scratch are dropped before the atomic write.

reviewFindings.title: Root status reports unobserved credential source
reviewFindings.detail: Mode 1 consumes an exact bounded no-follow source companion and root status reports the retained unit-configured source rather than inferring it from mode.

reviewFindings.title: Strong readiness lacks production-boundary tests
reviewFindings.detail: The real readiness branch is behind injectable production-identical hooks with empty/nonempty/failure/timeout side-effect assertions and child-process stdout/exit tests.

reviewFindings.title: Readiness and startup namespace classifiers diverge
reviewFindings.detail: Production Mode 1 startup and strong readiness now use the common exact classifier; broad startup marker parsers were removed.

reviewFindings.title: Verifier failure can leave partial stdout
reviewFindings.detail: Machine output requires pipe/FIFO stdout, validates PIPE_BUF, enables nonblocking mode, and uses one bounded write with full/EAGAIN/error tests.

reviewFindings.title: Verifier stderr is not globally bounded/redacted
reviewFindings.detail: Strong readiness branches before tracing and maps all failures to one bounded static redacted stderr line.

reviewFindings.title: SecurityInfo enums lack strict validation
reviewFindings.detail: Common strict validation converts every SecurityInfo enum and rejects malformed bounded fields and inconsistent Mode0/Mode1 vectors.

reviewFindings.title: Provisioning receipt hash lacks golden
reviewFindings.detail: A fixed receipt JSON fixture and SHA-256 now pin field order and schema-version stability.

reviewFindings.title: Strong readiness production boundary is not exercised
reviewFindings.detail: Actual main and tests now call the same DaemonRuntime-backed daemon_main entry. Runtime methods cover parse/deadline/identity/config/key/verifier/error/output and all normal side-effect gates; child tests assert exact call sequences and zero forbidden methods.

reviewFindings.title: Strong verifier panic bypasses stderr policy
reviewFindings.detail: A one-time global hook dispatches on an atomic verifier mode, writes one static redacted line, and the entire post-parse strong branch is catch_unwind-contained; child panic output is exact and stdout empty.

reviewFindings.title: Guard-page unmap test is flaky
reviewFindings.detail: The real partial guard failure and mincore observation execute consecutively in a single-threaded fork child, eliminating concurrent virtual-address reuse while retaining real wipe/unlock/munmap execution.

reviewFindings.title: Verifier panic containment is process-global
reviewFindings.detail: Synchronous process entry installs the dormant hook and parses arguments before Tokio construction. Strong mode runs on the initial thread with thread-local panic state and bounded static stderr writes; normal/header-only paths explicitly construct one multithread Tokio runtime.

reviewFindings.title: Verifier deadline excludes hook setup
reviewFindings.detail: Strong entry captures the absolute deadline origin as its first operation, before TLS arming or any first-use synchronization, and constructs the deadline from that origin inside containment.

reviewFindings.title: Real guarded cleanup test does not prove wipe and unlock
reviewFindings.detail: Cfg(test) instrumentation at the real disposal sequence observes all-zero key bytes before munlock, successful munlock, successful munmap, and immediate ENOMEM unmapped state in a single-threaded fork child.

reviewFindings.title: Recovery can leave enabled config unguarded
reviewFindings.detail: Recovery removes guard before fallible activation/restoration and returns errors without re-guarding/stopping; malformed journal also remains potentially live. Add fail-closed supervisor on every recovery error.

reviewFindings.title: Effective systemd guards and credentials are unverified
reviewFindings.detail: Engine hashes base service only and never validates effective service/socket guards or credential maps against overrides/drop-ins. Query/bind strict effective unit configuration.

reviewFindings.title: Provisioning state table is not enforced
reviewFindings.detail: Production hand logic bypasses common provisioning classifier; stale receipts can imply adoption/idempotence without readiness. Use common classifier and rerun strong readiness for all idempotent paths.

reviewFindings.title: Durable side effects precede transaction guard
reviewFindings.detail: Unit snapshots and systemd-creds occur before durable journal/guard; host secret may be created unjournaled. Journal/guard first, stop/settle/resnapshot, require or journal host secret setup.

reviewFindings.title: Unadopted artifact cleanup has TOCTOU
reviewFindings.detail: Cleanup is unguarded and treats malformed references as absent. Guard/stop/settle and revalidate all references/jobs/transients/daemon/artifact immediately before unlink.

reviewFindings.title: Activation can use objects changed after readiness
reviewFindings.detail: Enable/recovery does not reread artifact/drop-in/config/namespace after readiness. Revalidate every bound object immediately before activation and rerun readiness in activation recovery.

reviewFindings.title: Transient readiness cleanup violates deadline
reviewFindings.detail: Readiness timeout joins captures before stopping PID1-owned transient and cleanup can run for ~25 minutes. Use one absolute deadline, stop/kill unit before joins, bound every query.

reviewFindings.title: Disabled recovery loses unit restoration target
reviewFindings.detail: Disabled completion deletes guard/journal before prior unit restoration. Keep journal through guard removal, exact restore and verification; delete last.

reviewFindings.title: Transaction production adapters are unqualified
reviewFindings.detail: Production adapters differ materially from mocks and lack real syscall/process/systemd transport tests.

reviewFindings.title: Atomic exchange backup is not transaction-bound
reviewFindings.detail: Exchange uses predictable unjournaled backup names and target-check race. Use transaction-owned pre-recorded names and descriptor identity binding.

reviewFindings.title: Mode 0 remains coupled to Mode 1 credential
reviewFindings.detail: Mode0 leaves base pathless credential and can fail on corrupt Mode1 artifact. Install explicit Mode0 drop-in clearing credentials.

reviewFindings.title: Mode 0 status validation is weak
reviewFindings.detail: Mode0 activation status does not bind new invocation/build/binary/readiness. Apply strict root status contract.

reviewFindings.title: Credential output cap is not enforced live
reviewFindings.detail: Anonymous systemd-creds memfd ignores declared 1024-byte stdout cap until exit. Enforce cap during execution.

reviewFindings.title: Journal claims units stopped too early
reviewFindings.detail: UnitsStopped is persisted before stop operations; crash can leave running units while journal claims stopped. Persist guarded snapshot, stop+settle, then UnitsStopped.

reviewFindings.title: Fresh provisioning filesystem layout is absent
reviewFindings.detail: Fresh production path lacks required dirs/config 0600 and package/local paths do not create them. Transactionally create/validate exact directories and secure config.

reviewFindings.title: Different-mode artifact can be implicitly adopted
reviewFindings.detail: DifferentMode classifier bypasses artifact adoption; Mode0 config plus Mode1 artifact is silently adopted. Preserve artifact state and require explicit adopt+readiness.

reviewFindings.title: Disabled provisioning cannot restore active service
reviewFindings.detail: Disabled provision tries to restore service active, but disabled daemon exits success and service becomes inactive. Define safe disabled post-provision target preserving enablement/socket while service inactive.
reviewFindings.source: security engine unit restore; daemon disabled behavior

reviewFindings.title: Cleanup can delete newly adopted artifact
reviewFindings.detail: Cleanup reference checks race reference creation before unlink. Quarantine by transaction-owned rename, fsync, recheck all references/units, restore on conflict, then unlink.

reviewFindings.title: Release-N packaging bridge is not implemented
reviewFindings.detail: Release-N package/config ownership bridge and local installer integration are absent; systemd dependency still 257.

reviewFindings.title: Activation omits public status validation
reviewFindings.detail: Activation validates root status only, not public Info readiness/policy consistency.

reviewFindings.title: Real transaction integration coverage is partial
reviewFindings.detail: Real adapter tests do not execute full SecurityEngine against rooted production filesystem/unit lifecycle, hiding fresh/disabled gaps.

reviewFindings.title: Credential transport comment is stale
reviewFindings.detail: Transport comment still says memfd though bounded pipe is used.

reviewFindings.title: Bridge stash restoration can expose legacy config
reviewFindings.detail: Absent/present stashes do not safely restore across variant/reinstall and can suppress bootstrap while leaving enabled legacy config. Implement exact absent removal/present restore/refusal lifecycle.

reviewFindings.title: Removal can proceed after stash failure
reviewFindings.detail: pre_remove failure does not abort ALPM removal/variant switch; add PreTransaction Remove AbortOnFail hook.

reviewFindings.title: Fresh package bootstrap failure is fail-open
reviewFindings.detail: Fresh post_install failure leaves enabled legacy payload and may later start. Add persistent positive bootstrap-complete guard removed/created only after successful bootstrap.

reviewFindings.title: Bridge crash recovery misses control publication windows
reviewFindings.detail: Bridge control files/stages are not fully crash-atomic; orphan stage/partial JSON can be unrecoverable. Journal before stage and atomically publish controls with kill-window tests.

reviewFindings.title: Clean makepkg fails
reviewFindings.detail: prepare/build use different CARGO_HOME; clean frozen makepkg cannot resolve dependencies.

reviewFindings.title: Local installer stop order is unsafe
reviewFindings.detail: Local installer stops service before socket, allowing reactivation. Stop socket first, settle both.

reviewFindings.title: Local rollback can restart cached new units
reviewFindings.detail: Rollback restores old unit files after daemon-reload but does not reload manager before restart. Track reload and reload restored files; fail stopped on error.

reviewFindings.title: Bridge stash becomes permanently stale
reviewFindings.detail: Stash cannot refresh after administrator edits. Add crash-safe refresh/consume/reset lifecycle.

reviewFindings.title: Release bridge lacks ALPM-root tests
reviewFindings.detail: Package tests are function/file checks, not isolated ALPM transaction matrix.

reviewFindings.title: Local directory setup follows symlinks
reviewFindings.detail: Local install -d follows symlinked sensitive components. Use descriptor-safe shared helper.

reviewFindings.title: Local migration option is misleading
reviewFindings.detail: Migration flags are advertised but always rejected. Remove pseudo-options or implement safe post-transaction call.

reviewFindings.title: Local installer misclassifies occupied config
reviewFindings.detail: create-if-absent Occupied is treated as created. Return distinct result and verify descriptor before state/rollback.

reviewFindings.title: Package version metadata is stale
reviewFindings.detail: Package version metadata is stale relative to checkpoint.

reviewFindings.title: Bridge recovery can orphan restore stage
reviewFindings.detail: Restore path returns when target matches stash even if exchanged legacy stage remains, then removes journal/marks complete and detects orphan. Clean exact stage before journal/marker; add kill-window tests.

reviewFindings.title: Bootstrap marker condition breaks provisioning
reviewFindings.detail: Units now have positive bootstrap condition but effective policy/common contract accepts only negative transaction guard. Model/require both exact conditions and update hashes/tests.
reviewFindings.source: systemd units; security real parser; common provisioning

reviewFindings.title: Local fresh install lacks bootstrap marker
reviewFindings.detail: Fresh local install creates config but never bootstrap marker, so units cannot activate. Complete local marker transaction after verified config.

reviewFindings.title: Local installer still allows socket reactivation
reviewFindings.detail: Local installer still stops service before socket and lacks stable recheck.

reviewFindings.title: Local rollback can restart cached new units
reviewFindings.detail: Rollback restores old units without daemon-reload before restart.

reviewFindings.title: Release bridge lacks real ALPM matrix
reviewFindings.detail: Package test does not run actual isolated ALPM transactions or hook failures/backup/pacsave/variant/N+1 matrix.

reviewFindings.title: Local layout/result handling is unsafe
reviewFindings.detail: Local sensitive dirs use install -d and Created/Occupied are conflated.

reviewFindings.title: Bridge named temporary leaks on collision
reviewFindings.detail: Named temporary fallback remains on Occupied publication collision.

reviewFindings.title: Package metadata version is stale
reviewFindings.detail: .SRCINFO versions differ from PKGBUILD.

reviewFindings.title: Installer usage is misleading
reviewFindings.detail: Installer usage advertises rejected migrate option.

reviewFindings.title: Bridge formatting is not clean
reviewFindings.detail: New bridge files fail rustfmt check.

notes: Research completed 2026-07-12: systemd credential/userspace AEAD is the recommended production backend; TPM Trusted Key plus AF_ALG is viable only as an experimental plaintext-reduced backend because AF_ALG is deprecated and slower than optimized userspace crypto.

Research completed 2026-07-12: prompt-before-capture confirmation is the recommended UX. Fixed Enter/OK is preferred over random challenges; an opaque one-use protocol token provides transaction freshness.

Mode 2 should overlap request-local decrypt/decode/validation with camera startup after confirmation and camera admission. Both branches must join before matching, and plaintext must be zeroized on every exit path.
A common AES-256-GCM record format is preferred for modes 1 and 2 because the kernel AF_ALG backend supports it. The format retains an algorithm identifier for future migrations.
Existing uncommitted repository changes and reports must not be overwritten or committed as part of this work without explicit approval.

Superseding earlier research notes: AF_ALG is rejected and removed from implementation scope. Mode 2 uses a `user`-type key linked only into the systemd-created private session keyring, transient `keyctl_read`, and userspace AES-GCM.
